knowledge_objects.name: Statistical Model
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A family of probability distributions indexed by an unknown parameter.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A statistical model is a family of probability distributions indexed by an unknown parameter \(\theta\).

knowledge_objects.name: Likelihood
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A function defined from the statistical model by treating observations as fixed and comparing possible parameter values.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The likelihood is defined from this statistical model by treating the observations as fixed and comparing possible parameter values.

knowledge_objects.name: Likelihood Function
knowledge_objects.short_definition: For independent observations, the product of the densities evaluated at each observation.

knowledge_objects.name: Log-Likelihood
knowledge_objects.source_span: The log-likelihood is obtained by taking the logarithm of the likelihood.

knowledge_objects.name: Log-Likelihood Function
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Sum of log-densities for independent observations.

knowledge_objects.name: Maximum Likelihood Estimation
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A method that selects a parameter value maximizing the log-likelihood.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Maximum likelihood estimation uses the log-likelihood as its objective and selects a parameter value that maximizes it

knowledge_objects.name: MLE Estimator
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The parameter value that maximizes the log-likelihood.

knowledge_objects.name: Score Function
knowledge_objects.short_definition: The derivative of the log-likelihood with respect to the parameter.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The score function is the derivative of the log-likelihood with respect to the parameter

knowledge_objects.name: Score Equation
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Equation s(θ)=0 that an MLE candidate satisfies at an interior differentiable optimum.
knowledge_objects.source_span: At an interior differentiable optimum, an MLE candidate satisfies the score equation \(s(\theta)=0\).

knowledge_objects.name: Newton's Method
knowledge_objects.short_definition: An iterative numerical method applied to compute an MLE candidate when the score equation has no closed-form solution.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Newton's method can be applied within maximum likelihood estimation to compute a candidate numerically.
